title: Having vehicle warranty problems with an LDV T60 how do i submit a warranty claim to ldv

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Having vehicle warranty problems with an LDV T60 how do i submit a warranty claim to ldv

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
To submit a warranty claim for your LDV T60, follow these steps:

1. Locate your nearest LDV dealership: To find your nearest LDV dealership, visit the LDV website (www.ldvautomotive.com.au) and click on "Dealer Locator" to find your nearest dealer. Alternatively, search for "LDV dealership [your location]" on Google.

2. Contact the dealership: Call or visit the dealership and let them know that you are experiencing issues with your vehicle and would like to submit a warranty claim. They should be able to guide you through the process.

3. Provide required information: The dealership may ask for your vehicle identification number (VIN), date of purchase, odometer reading, and details of the issues you are experiencing. Make sure to have this information handy when contacting them.

4. Schedule an appointment: The dealership will schedule an appointment for you to bring your vehicle in for inspection and potential repairs under warranty.

5. Bring your vehicle to the dealership: On the day of your appointment, bring your vehicle to the dealership. They will inspect the vehicle to determine if the issues are covered under warranty and provide you with an estimate for repair.

6. Repairs and claim submission: If the issues are covered under warranty, the dealership will perform the necessary repairs at no cost to you. They will also submit the warranty claim to LDV on your behalf.

7. Follow up: Keep in contact with the dealership to ensure everything goes smoothly with the repair and warranty claim process. They should keep you informed of any updates or further steps needed.

Remember to keep any receipts or documentation related to the vehicle issue and warranty claim just in case future issues arise.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: the dealer refuses to  assist what do i do next.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
If the dealer refuses to assist with your warranty claim, you can take the following steps:

1. Contact LDV Customer Support: Reach out to LDV customer support directly through their website or by phone. Explain the situation and provide details of your warranty claim, vehicle issues, and the dealer's refusal to assist. They may be able to guide you through the next steps or help resolve the issue.

Phone number (in Australia): 1800 874 782
E-mail: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>

2. Gather documentation: Collect any relevant documents, such as warranty information, invoices, receipts, and correspondence with the dealership, as this information may be vital in proving your case.

3. Contact another LDV dealership: If there is another LDV dealership in your area, you can try contacting them to see if they are willing to assist with your warranty claim.

4. Submit a complaint: If the dealer's refusal to assist is due to poor service or unethical practices, you can submit a complaint to consumer protection agencies in your area. In Australia, this could be the Australian Competition & Consumer Commission (ACCC) or the consumer affairs department of your state or territory.

5. Seek legal advice: If all else fails, consider seeking legal advice from a lawyer or consumer advocate who specializes in warranty and consumer law. They can guide you on your rights and potential legal recourse in this situation.

Remember to stay polite and persistent, as resolving warranty disputes may take time and require collaboration between the parties involved.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The vehicle was purchased in NSW Australia who are the authorise here that can assist me

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
In New South Wales, Australia, the organization responsible for consumer protection, including automotive warranty issues, is NSW Fair Trading. If you need assistance with your LDV vehicle warranty claim, you can follow these steps:

1. Contact NSW Fair Trading: You can lodge a complaint or seek advice by visiting their website (www.fairtrading.nsw.gov.au) or by calling their helpline.

Phone: 13 32 20
Online complaint form: https://www.fairtrading.nsw.gov.au/help-centre/online-tools/make-a-complaint

2. Provide relevant information: When submitting your complaint or seeking advice, ensure you provide all necessary details such as your vehicle identification number (VIN), warranty information, details of the issue(s), and the interaction you had with the dealership.

3. Follow the process advised by NSW Fair Trading: They may advise you to attempt resolution through negotiation with the dealer, offer mediation services, or provide guidance on escalating the matter further. Follow the process and advice provided by the agency.

4. Consider seeking legal advice: If your situation does not improve, you may want to consult a lawyer who specializes in consumer law to provide guidance on your rights and the best course of action.

Remember to keep all relevant documentation and correspondence throughout the process in case it is needed for reference or evidence.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
{The following  is the list of the ongoing issues with my car that are covered under warranty and require urgent attention, as they are impacting my safety while driving and causing me immense stress and anxiety:

The extremely poor-quality camera/video image display is making driving dangerous, easy to have collisions with road hazards and pedestrians.
The phone apps (such as Waze and Google Maps) which display in the screen are of poor-quality image making it difficult to view and making driving very dangerous.
The blinker control function, car camera, and GPS app systems malfunction when the blinker turns on and off, causing loss of GPS direction and recurring change of screen display making driving dangerous and causing road hazard risks.
The settings which have been saved (in the display unit) frequently and persistently keep resetting to factory or other settings, losing the preferred saved settings causing immense frustration and making it difficult to drive the car safely.
Defective rain sensors not turning on or turning on when it is not raining, causing immense frustration.
Defective intermittent speed function of the wipers, causing frustration, and not working most of the time now.
Extremely poor-quality AM radio reception, causing extreme frustration which is not acceptable.
Persistent and repeated failure of Bluetooth phone connection. Reverting to factory or other settings, causing me immense frustration and making driving dangerous as I cannot answer or make calls without stopping the car and reconnecting.
Saved phone numbers and contacts keep disappearing, causing intense grief, and making driving conditions dangerous as I am unable to use the phone. I Need to stop the car to use the phone.
Defective clock display, continuously not keeping time, causing frustration.
Volume settings resetting upon every ignition, which is annoying.
The display screen, GPS and display control turns black for a long time at random and when passing beneath an overpass bridge. Making it impossible to read the display screen, check my speed, or anything else, causing immediate and persistent stress and placing me at an increased risk of collision. Needing to stop or slow down because you are driving blind. 
The entertainment system displaying phone apps and playing music randomly disconnects and goes blank when scrolling through the display board system or while using the GPS, causing frustration and making driving dangerous. I need to stop the car and restart the apps before the screen display reappears.
Cruise control not working correctly, disengaging when driving on the highway, causing frustration and an unsafe road risk.
The transmission is not smooth as it used to be, causing the vehicle to jolt and slip, with the change in the gear shift happening unexpectedly, causing uncertainty as it seems the vehicle will break down.
Lag during take-off when I press the accelerator, especially in heavy traffic with frequent stop-start driving, causing uncertainty as if the vehicle will not move and cause me to have an accident.
The repaired roller cover latch fitting has not been correctly aligned with the roller cover, which makes it very hard and difficult to open.
As all these issues are covered under warranty, I am requesting that the defects be fixed urgently, as they are long overdue, and it has become unsafe for me to drive the car. I have experienced many close calls of having accidents on the road due to the cars manufacturing faults, which have nearly caused damage to property and injury to people on multiple occasions. This situation is unacceptable and cannot continue. I cannot live like this.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
I understand how frustrating and concerning these issues can be, especially when they impact your safety and driving experience. Given that these problems are covered under warranty, you should follow these steps to seek resolution:

1. Document the issues: Write down all the issues you listed, including when they started and how they are affecting your safety and driving experience. If possible, take photos or record videos as evidence for your claims.

2. Contact your LDV dealership: Reach out to the LDV dealership where you purchased the vehicle and provide them with the list of documented issues. Request an appointment with the dealership's service department to inspect and resolve the warranty-covered issues.

3. Contact LDV Australia customer support: If the dealer is unhelpful, you can reach out to LDV Australia's customer support directly. Explain the situation, including the details of the issues and your dissatisfaction with the dealer's response.

Phone number (in Australia): 1800 874 782
E-mail: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>

4. Contact NSW Fair Trading: If the dealer and LDV Australia customer support are unable to assist adequately, you can lodge a complaint with NSW Fair Trading.

Phone: 13 32 20
Online complaint form: https://www.fairtrading.nsw.gov.au/help-centre/online-tools/make-a-complaint

5. Seek legal advice: If all else fails, consider consulting a lawyer or consumer advocate who specializes in warranty and consumer law. They can guide you on your rights and potential legal recourse to ensure the safety issues are addressed as per your warranty coverage.

Remember, communication between you and the dealership, LDV Australia, and any involved agencies should be polite, assertive, and focused on resolving the warranty-covered issues as rapidly and efficiently as possible.

turns.text:
Subject: Request for Assistance with Warranty Issues on My LDV Vehicle

Dear LDV Customer Support,

I am writing to request your assistance with several ongoing issues concerning my [vehicle model and year, e.g., LDV T60 2020], which are covered under warranty but have not been resolved by the dealership where I purchased the vehicle. These problems are seriously impacting my safety and driving experience, causing extreme frustration and anxiety.

Below is a list of the warranty-covered issues I am experiencing:

[Include the list of issues you provided in the previous question here]

I have attempted to address these issues with my local LDV dealership, but their response and assistance have been unsatisfactory. As the issues remain unresolved, I am seeking your intervention to ensure that these defects are fixed urgently as per the warranty coverage.

I kindly request your guidance on the next steps for having these issues inspected and resolved. Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]

---

Subject: Complaint Regarding LDV Dealership and Unresolved Warranty Issues

Dear NSW Fair Trading,

I am writing to file a complaint against [LDV dealership name and address] regarding the unresolved warranty issues on my [vehicle model and year, e.g., LDV T60 2020]. Despite repeated attempts to have the dealership resolve the problems, the issues remain unaddressed, severely impacting my safety and driving experience.

Below is a list of the warranty-covered issues I have encountered with my vehicle:

[Include the list of issues you provided in the previous question here]

I have contacted both the LDV dealership and LDV Australia customer support seeking assistance, but my concerns have not been adequately addressed, leaving me with unresolved safety issues and a deteriorating driving experience.

I kindly request that NSW Fair Trading investigate the matter and take appropriate action to ensure that the warranty issues with my vehicle are resolved promptly. I am happy to provide any additional information or evidence as required.

Thank you in advance for your assistance and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Contact Information]
